table_semantics.justification: Full table structure: <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, and all header cells use <th scope='col'>. <time datetime> is used for all date values. Table is used only for tabular data, not layout.

element_choice.justification: All interactive elements are native:

form_semantics.justification: Every input and select has a visible <label> with correct for/id association. All filter controls are grouped inside a <search> landmark. Native <select> elements used for department and status filters. Minor: 'Apply filters' uses type='submit' inside <search> without a wrapping <form>, so the button has no form to submit, but element choices and labeling are otherwise correct.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used sparingly and appropriately: aria-label on both <nav> elements and <search>, aria-current='page' on both the active nav link and the current pagination page, aria-disabled='true' on the disabled Previous link. No redundant roles (no role='navigation' on <nav>, no role='button' on <button>).

heading_hierarchy.justification:
Single

for the page title. No subheadings needed and none added. 'Showing 8 of 42' is correctly a

, not a heading.

landmark_structure.justification: Full landmark set: <header>, <main id='main-content'>, <footer>, labelled <nav> for primary navigation and pagination, and <search> for filter controls. Two skip links present targeting main content and the search element.

content_realism.justification: Eight rows of diverse, realistic employee data with varied names, departments, roles, dates, and all three status values represented. No placeholder content.

list_semantics.justification: Navigation items and pagination items are both wrapped in <ul> within their respective <nav> elements, correctly conveying count and grouping.

expectations_met: Status column content is not conveyed by colour alone — text labels used exclusively
Department and status filters use native <select> elements
Filter controls are grouped in a <search> element
Pagination indicates current page with aria-current='page'
Table is not wrapped in role='grid'
No table semantics stripped by CSS display properties
additional_observations: <time datetime> used for all eight date values — excellent element_choice detail beyond the minimum
aria-disabled='true' on the Previous link when on page 1 is a thoughtful accessibility touch
<button type='submit'> inside <search> without a wrapping <form> means the submit type has no target form; type='button' would be more precise, but this is a very minor issue
Skip links provided for both main content and the search landmark, well-matched to page complexity
